raw_consumer_statement:
I am disputing a charge from XXXX ( {$1500.00}, reference XXXX ) for hotel services that were never provided. I never checked in to the hotel, and I provided American Express with multiple pieces of supporting evidence, including : Confirmation emails from XX/XX/XXXX that I did not check in. 
XX/XX/XXXXscreenshots showing the reservation was canceled. 
Proof that the rooms remained available for resale on the stay dates. 
Parallel evidence from another dispute ( XXXX ) with the same hotel, dates, and terms, which American Express resolved in my favor and refunded. 
Despite this, American Express denied dispute XXXX, citing only the merchants cancellation policy. This is inconsistent, because the other dispute was credited even though the circumstances were identical. 
I specifically requested from American Express explanation justifying the charge, including proof of services provided and the rationale for denying my refund despite no stay and available resale of the rooms. Instead, they only repeated the cancellation policy, which does not address the fact that no services were rendered or why my two disputes were handled differently.

I believe this constitutes unfair and inconsistent handling of disputes under the Fair Credit Billing Act. American Express has not provided a reasonable explanation or consistent resolution. I am requesting that this matter be fully reviewed, that the denial of XXXX be reversed, and that I be credited for the {$1500.00} charged for services never received.
